content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance Administration          Executive Legal          Enforcement          Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                              322 5901                322 6441              222 6444


November 16, 1977


Phillip E. Blecher
Public Utilities Commission
State of California
California State Building
San Francisco, California  94102

ATTENTION:  Mr. Tom MacBride

Dear Mr. Blecher:

     This is in response to your letter to Mr. Bennett dated
9 November 1977, requesting him to deem the deletion of Section
26 of the Conflict of Interest Code of the Public Utilities
Commission non-substantive in nature within the meaning of
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752. On behalf of the Executive
Director, it is the staff's conclusion that the proposed alter-
ation referred to in your letter is non-substantive within the
meaning of Section (d)(3) of the cited Regulation, and, accord-
ingly, the Public Utilities Commission may make the suggested
alteration without complying with Government Code Sections
87311 and 87303 at this time.

     In light of the fact that your Conflict of Interest Code
does not take effect until January 1, 1978, it is our further
conclusion that the cited alteration should be one deemed made
in calendar year 1978 and hence the review of such alteration
pursuant to the procedure described in subsection (f) of the
Regulation need not be accomplished until not later than April
of 1979.

     I trust this resolves your problem. Thank you for writing.
If I can be of further assistance in this or any other matter,
please feel free to call on me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa

PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

UTILITIES ALL COMMUNICATION
TO THE COMMISSION
SAN FRANCISCO BUILDING
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA
TEL: (415) 557-2444

November 9, 1977

Mr. Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Mr. Kenneth Goshorn

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Please find enclosed an "Order Reopening and Setting Hearing"
in Case No. 10084. The Public Utilities Commission on
October 25, 1977 issued this order in order to reconsider
the appropriateness of Section 26 of the Commission's Conflict
of Interest Code (see footnote No. 2 of the order).

As pointed out in the order, the promulgation of Section 26
was not required by the Political Reform Act. Further, it is
clear that the deletion of Section 26 from our Code would not
change the disclosure or disqualification responsibilities of
any designated employee. We therefore seek your approval,
pursuant to 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18732(c), to classify
the proposed deletion or modification of Section 26 as a
non-substantive alteration within the meaning of 2 Cal. Admin.
Code Section 18755.(d)(3).

We intend to make any such alteration effective on January 1,
1978 which is also the effective date of our Conflict of
Interest Code. Since it is likely that substantive changes
to the Code will be proposed during 1978, we will submit
to 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18732(c), comply with Government
Code Section 87311 with respect to this non-substantive altera-
tion at the same time that compliance with respect to any
proposed substantive changes is effected. In the unlikely
event that no substantive changes are proposed in the first
half of 1978, we will nonetheless take steps to effect compliance
with Government Code Section 87311 in order to meet the deadline
imposed by 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18752(b).

Mr. Bennett                    -2-                    November 9, 1977


Should you have any questions regarding this request, please
contact Mr. MacBride at AISS 597-2442.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Phillip L. Blecher
Executive Director

Enclosure

sections.question: May the Public Utilities Commission delete Section 26 of its Conflict of Interest Code and have such deletion deemed non-substantive in nature within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752?
sections.conclusion: It is the staff's conclusion that the proposed alteration referred to in your letter is non-substantive within the meaning of Section (d)(3) of the cited Regulation, and, accordingly, the Public Utilities Commission may make the suggested alteration without complying with Government Code Sections 87311 and 87303 at this time. Further, the cited alteration should be deemed made in calendar year 1978 and the review of such alteration need not be accomplished until not later than April of 1979.
sections.question_synthetic: The Public Utilities Commission requested FPPC approval to classify the deletion of Section 26 from its Conflict of Interest Code as a non-substantive alteration under the applicable administrative code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC approved the deletion of Section 26 as a non-substantive alteration, permitting the Public Utilities Commission to implement it without complying with certain Government Code sections immediately. The review deadline for this alteration was extended to April 1979 since the Code takes effect January 1, 1978.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated November 9, 1977) and FPPC response (dated November 16, 1977). The question and conclusion were synthesized from the incoming request and response letter respectively, as no formal Q&C sections were explicitly labeled.

QUESTION: May the Public Utilities Commission delete Section 26 of its Conflict of Interest Code and have such deletion deemed non-substantive in nature within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752?

CONCLUSION: It is the staff's conclusion that the proposed alteration referred to in your letter is non-substantive within the meaning of Section (d)(3) of the cited Regulation, and, accordingly, the Public Utilities Commission may make the suggested alteration without complying with Government Code Sections 87311 and 87303 at this time. Further, the cited alteration should be deemed made in calendar year 1978 and the review of such alteration need not be accomplished until not later than April of 1979.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC response approving the Public Utilities Commission's request to delete Section 26 from its Conflict of Interest Code as a non-substantive alteration. The deletion is approved without immediate compliance with certain Government Code sections, with review required by April 1979.
